 ```\n187\t\n188\t2. **Verify Pluck strand is active:**\n189\t   ```bash\n190\t   grep \"worker booted\" pluck-debug.log | grep \"pluck\"\n191\t   ```\n192\t\n193\t3. **Ensure beads are available for The setup provides multiple preset configurations ranging from minimal logging to full trace-level output.\n\n## Configuration Script\n\nThe main configuration script is located at:\n```\n/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh\n```\n\nThis script provides preset configurations for different debug levels and handles log capture automatically.\n\n## Available Debug Presets\n\n### 1. **minimal** - INFO level\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=info`\n- **Output:** High-level strand operations only\n- **Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification\n\n### 2. **standard** - DEBUG level (Recommended)\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=debug`\n- **Output:** Filtering decisions and statistics\n- **Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior\n\n### 3. **detailed** - TRACE level\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace`\n- **Output:** Complete execution details\n- **Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow\n\n### 4. **comprehensive** - Multi-module TRACE\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug`\n- **Output:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG\n- **Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions\n\n### 5. **full** - All NEEDLE modules\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug`\n- **Output:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level\n- **Use case:** Complete system debugging\n\n### 6. **maximum** - Global TRACE\n- **RUST_LOG:** `trace`\n- **Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)\n- **Use case:** Deep system-level debugging\n\n## Usage\n\n### Basic Usage\n\n```bash\n# Run with standard debug level (recommended)\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n\n# Run with comprehensive debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n\n# Run with detailed trace level\n./crawl-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n```\n\n### Advanced Usage\n\n```bash\n# Specify workspace, output file, mode, and run count\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR custom-output.log full 3\n\n# Show help\n./pluck-debug-config.sh --help\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output\n\nWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled, you should see the following events:\n\n### 1. Strand Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n### 3. Query Results\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by excluded labels\n  excluded_beads=[\"bf-1234\", \"bf-5678\"]\n  reasons=[\"label:deferred\", \"label:blocked\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by status and assignee\n  remaining=3\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting candidates by priority\n  first_candidate=\"bf-abcd\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Decision\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split threshold\n  failure_count=2\n  split_threshold=3\n  should_split=false\n```\n\n### 8. Final Result\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Strand evaluation complete\n  result=BeadFound(\"bf-abcd\")\n```\n\n## Manual Configuration\n\nIf you prefer to set up the environment manually:\n\n```bash\n# Set the desired debug level\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n\n# Run NEEDLE\ncd /home/coding/NEEDLE\ncargo run -- run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n\n# Or with output capture\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Log Analysis\n\nAfter capturing logs, analyze them with these commands:\n\n```bash\n# View all Pluck-related events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Filter specific decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# View candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Count events by type\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Pluck output visible\n\n1. **Check RUST_LOG is set correctly:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Verify Pluck strand is active:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"worker booted\" pluck-debug.log | grep \"pluck\"\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure beads are available for processing:**\n   ```bash\n   br list --status=open\n   ```\n\n### Bead store query failed\n\nThis error indicates the Pluck strand cannot access the bead store:\n```\nERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed error=bf list failed\n```\n\n**Possible causes:**\n- Bead store locked by another process\n- Corrupted bead database\n- Permission issues\n\n**Resolution:**\n```bash\n# Check bead store integrity\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nbr doctor --repair\n```\n\n## Configuration Files\n\n### Script Configuration\nThe main configuration is in the script itself:\n- **Location:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh`\n- **Configuration:** Lines 20-27 contain the preset definitions\n\n### Environment Variables\n- **RUST_LOG:** Controls Rust crate-level logging\n- **RUST_BACKTRACE:** Set to `1` for backtraces on errors\n\n## Integration with NEEDLE\n\nThe Pluck strand is part of the standard NEEDLE strand set:\n```\nstrands=[\"pluck\", \"mend\", \"explore\", \"weave\", \"unravel\", \"pulse\", \"reflect\", \"splice\", \"knot\"]\n```\n\nPluck is the first strand evaluated and is responsible for:\n1. Querying the bead store for ready beads\n2. Filtering by labels (deferred, human, blocked)\n3. Filtering by assignee\n4. Sorting candidates by priority\n5. Checking for split conditions\n6. Returning NoWork/BeadFound/Split result\n\n## Next Steps\n\nOnce debug logging is configured:\n\n1. **Run a test capture:**\n   ```bash\n   ./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR test-output.log standard\n   ```\n\n2. **Analyze the output:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" test-output.log\n   ```\n\n3. **Filter for specific decisions:**\n   ```bash\n   grep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" test-output.log\n   ```\n\n4. **Verify expected behavior:**\n   - Check that excluded labels are properly filtered\n   - Verify split threshold logic\n   - Confirm candidate selection order\n\n## Status\n\n✅ **Debug logging infrastructure:** Configured and operational  \n✅ **Configuration script:** Created and executable  \n✅ **Preset configurations:** 6 levels available  \n✅ **Documentation:** Complete  \n✅ **Ready for execution:** Yes  \n\nThe Pluck debug logging configuration is complete and ready for use.","numLines":275,"startLine":1,"totalLines":275}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2f6817d1be754efd8c0a55c6","type":"tool_result","content":"![NEEDLE](docs/hero.png)\n\n# 🧵 NEEDLE\n\n[![CI](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.75+-orange.svg)](rust-toolchain.toml)\n[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-0.2.8-green.svg)](Cargo.toml)\n\n**N**avigates **E**very **E**nqueued **D**eliverable, **L**ogs **E**ffort\n\n> Deterministic bead processing with explicit outcome paths.\n\nNEEDLE is a universal wrapper for headless coding CLI agents. It processes a shared bead queue in deterministic order, dispatching work to any headless CLI (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Aider) and handling every outcome through an explicit, predefined path.\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quickstart\n\n```bash\n# Install the latest release\ncurl -fsSL https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/releases/latest/download/install.sh | bash\n\n# Or build from source\ncargo install --git https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE\n\n# Run a worker against a bead-tracked workspace\ncd /path/to/your/workspace\nneedle run --agent claude --identity alpha\n```\n\nA worker starts, claims the next bead, dispatches to your chosen agent CLI, and loops. Multiple workers can run in parallel against the same workspace — coordination is handled by the shared bead queue (no central orchestrator).\n\nSee [`docs/examples/`](docs/examples/) for end-to-end configurations.\n\n---\n\n## 🧶 What is a bead?\n\nA **bead** is a work item — the unit of work NEEDLE processes. Think of it as a structured task ticket: a title, a body describing the deliverable and acceptance criteria, a status (`open`, `in_progress`, `done`), and optional metadata like priority and dependencies.\n\nBeads live in a **bead store** — a SQLite database (`beads.db`) plus a JSONL checkpoint file (`issues.jsonl`) in a `.beads/` directory inside your project. The `br` CLI manages the bead store: create beads, list them, claim them, close them.\n\n```bash\n# Create a bead\nbr create --title \"Add pagination to search results\" --body \"...\"\n\n# List open beads\nbr list --status open\n\n# NEEDLE does the rest: claims, dispatches, and closes beads automatically\n```\n\nNEEDLE workers read from this store, claim the next available bead atomically, dispatch it to your chosen agent CLI, and close it on success — then loop.\n\n---\n\n## 🤔 Why NEEDLE\n\nExisting agent orchestration tools are built for one of two shapes:\n\n- **Conversational frameworks** (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) assume a chat loop with a human-in-the-loop or another LLM. They are bad at headless, long-running, cost-bounded work.\n- **Workflow engines** (Temporal, Argo Workflows, Inngest) assume each step is deterministic code. They are bad at non-deterministic agent steps whose outcomes have to be classified and routed.\n\nNEEDLE is the missing middle: a **deterministic state machine that drives non-deterministic agents.** Every outcome an agent can produce has an explicit handler. The agent's work is fuzzy; the orchestration around it is not.\n\n---\n\n## 🧠 Core Principle\n\nNEEDLE is a **state machine**, not a script. Every bead transitions through a finite set of states, and every transition has a defined handler. There are no implicit fallbacks, no swallowed errors, no undefined paths.\n\n```\nIf an outcome can happen, it has a handler.\nIf it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 The NEEDLE Algorithm\n\nA single worker executes this loop indefinitely:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                                                     │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │  🔍 SELECT │◄────────────────────────────────┐  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                  │  │\n│         │                                        │  │\n│         ▼                                        │  │\n│   ┌───────────┐   race lost    ┌──────────┐     │  │\n│   │  🔒 CLAIM  │──────────────►│ 🔁 RETRY  │─────┘  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘               └──────────┘        │\n│         │ claimed                                   │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ 📋 BUILD   │                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"33241d97-a2a2-4fd9-8670-1af9b709c49f","uuid":"59de28e9-755b-41db-91a5-2479981614b3","timestamp":"2026-07-09T09:18:37.317Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"![NEEDLE](docs/hero.png)\n\n# 🧵 NEEDLE\n\n[![CI](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.75+-orange.svg)](rust-toolchain.toml)\n[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-0.2.8-green.svg)](Cargo.toml)\n\n**N**avigates **E**very **E**nqueued **D**eliverable, **L**ogs **E**ffort\n\n> Deterministic bead processing with explicit outcome paths.\n\nNEEDLE is a universal wrapper for headless coding CLI agents. It processes a shared bead queue in deterministic order, dispatching work to any headless CLI (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Aider) and handling every outcome through an explicit, predefined path.\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quickstart\n\n```bash\n# Install the latest release\ncurl -fsSL https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/releases/latest/download/install.sh | bash\n\n# Or build from source\ncargo install --git https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE\n\n# Run a worker against a bead-tracked workspace\ncd /path/to/your/workspace\nneedle run --agent claude --identity alpha\n```\n\nA worker starts, claims the next bead, dispatches to your chosen agent CLI, and loops. Multiple workers can run in parallel against the same workspace — coordination is handled by the shared bead queue (no central orchestrator).\n\nSee [`docs/examples/`](docs/examples/) for end-to-end configurations.\n\n---\n\n## 🧶 What is a bead?\n\nA **bead** is a work item — the unit of work NEEDLE processes. Think of it as a structured task ticket: a title, a body describing the deliverable and acceptance criteria, a status (`open`, `in_progress`, `done`), and optional metadata like priority and dependencies.\n\nBeads live in a **bead store** — a SQLite database (`beads.db`) plus a JSONL checkpoint file (`issues.jsonl`) in a `.beads/` directory inside your project. The `br` CLI manages the bead store: create beads, list them, claim them, close them.\n\n```bash\n# Create a bead\nbr create --title \"Add pagination to search results\" --body \"...\"\n\n# List open beads\nbr list --status open\n\n# NEEDLE does the rest: claims, dispatches, and closes beads automatically\n```\n\nNEEDLE workers read from this store, claim the next available bead atomically, dispatch it to your chosen agent CLI, and close it on success — then loop.\n\n---\n\n## 🤔 Why NEEDLE\n\nExisting agent orchestration tools are built for one of two shapes:\n\n- **Conversational frameworks** (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) assume a chat loop with a human-in-the-loop or another LLM. They are bad at headless, long-running, cost-bounded work.\n- **Workflow engines** (Temporal, Argo Workflows, Inngest) assume each step is deterministic code. They are bad at non-deterministic agent steps whose outcomes have to be classified and routed.\n\nNEEDLE is the missing middle: a **deterministic state machine that drives non-deterministic agents.** Every outcome an agent can produce has an explicit handler. The agent's work is fuzzy; the orchestration around it is not.\n\n---\n\n## 🧠 Core Principle\n\nNEEDLE is a **state machine**, not a script. Every bead transitions through a finite set of states, and every transition has a defined handler. There are no implicit fallbacks, no swallowed errors, no undefined paths.\n\n```\nIf an outcome can happen, it has a handler.\nIf it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 The NEEDLE Algorithm\n\nA single worker executes this loop indefinitely:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                                                     │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │  🔍 SELECT │◄────────────────────────────────┐  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                  │  │\n│         │                                        │  │\n│         ▼                                        │  │\n│   ┌───────────┐   race lost    ┌──────────┐     │  │\n│   │  🔒 CLAIM  │──────────────►│ 🔁 RETRY  │─────┘  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘               └──────────┘        │\n│         │ claimed                                   │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ 📋 BUILD   │                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

This document provides the complete command structure for running Pluck with debug logging enabled.\n\n## Core Command Structure\n\n### Base Command\n\n```bash\nneedle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n### With Debug Logging\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> 2>&1 | tee <output_file>\n```\n\n## Command Options\n\n### needle run Options\n\n| Option | Short | Parameter | Description | Default |\n|--------|-------|-----------|-------------|---------|\n| `--workspace` | `-w` | `<PATH>` | Workspace directory to process beads from | Required |\n| `--agent` | `-a` | `<NAME>` | Agent adapter to use | Config default |\n| `--count` | `-c` | `<NUMBER>` | Number of workers to launch | 1 |\n| `--identifier` | `-i` | `<ID>` | Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming) | Auto-generated |\n| `--timeout` | `-t` | `<SECONDS>` | Agent execution timeout | Config default |\n| `--resume` | | | Resume existing worker session | false |\n| `--hot-reload` | | `true\\|false` | Enable hot-reload for this worker | Config default |\n\n### Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Examples |\n|----------|---------|----------|\n| `RUST_LOG` | Controls Rust crate-level logging | `needle::strand::pluck=debug` |\n| `RUST_BACKTRACE` | Enable backtraces on errors | `1` |\n\n## Debug Levels (RUST_LOG Settings)\n\n### 1. minimal - INFO Level\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n```\n**Purpose:** High-level strand operations only  \n**Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification  \n**Output:** Evaluation start/end, final results only\n\n### 2. standard - DEBUG Level (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n**Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior  \n**Output:** All filtering operations, candidate counts, decisions\n\n### 3. detailed - TRACE Level\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Complete execution details  \n**Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow  \n**Output:** Every step of the decision process with full details\n\n### 4. comprehensive - Multi-module TRACE\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG  \n**Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions  \n**Output:** Pluck trace + bead store queries, worker lifecycle\n\n### 5. full - All NEEDLE modules\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n**Use case:** Complete system debugging  \n**Output:** All major NEEDLE subsystems\n\n### 6. maximum - Global TRACE\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Everything at TRACE level  \n**Use case:** Deep system-level debugging  \n**Output:** All Rust modules at TRACE (very verbose)\n\n## Complete Command Examples\n\n### Basic Example (Standard Debug)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### With Comprehensive Logging\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n### With Multiple Workers\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 3 2>&1 | tee pluck-multi-worker.log\n```\n\n### With Agent Specification\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -a claude -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-claude.log\n```\n\n### With Timeout\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -t 300 -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-timeout.log\n```\n\n### With Custom Identifier\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -i \"debug-worker-1\" -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-custom.log\n```\n\n## Using the Configuration Script\n\nA convenience script is available at `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh`:\n\n```bash\n# Standard debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log standard\n\n# Comprehensive debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log comprehensive\n\n# Detailed trace level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log detailed\n\n# Full system debugging\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log full\n\n# Maximum verbosity\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log maximum\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output\n\n### 1. This document provides the complete command structure for running Pluck with debug logging enabled.\n\n## Core Command Structure\n\n### Base Command\n\n```bash\nneedle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n### With Debug Logging\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> 2>&1 | tee <output_file>\n```\n\n## Command Options\n\n### needle run Options\n\n| Option | Short | Parameter | Description | Default |\n|--------|-------|-----------|-------------|---------|\n| `--workspace` | `-w` | `<PATH>` | Workspace directory to process beads from | Required |\n| `--agent` | `-a` | `<NAME>` | Agent adapter to use | Config default |\n| `--count` | `-c` | `<NUMBER>` | Number of workers to launch | 1 |\n| `--identifier` | `-i` | `<ID>` | Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming) | Auto-generated |\n| `--timeout` | `-t` | `<SECONDS>` | Agent execution timeout | Config default |\n| `--resume` | | | Resume existing worker session | false |\n| `--hot-reload` | | `true\\|false` | Enable hot-reload for this worker | Config default |\n\n### Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Examples |\n|----------|---------|----------|\n| `RUST_LOG` | Controls Rust crate-level logging | `needle::strand::pluck=debug` |\n| `RUST_BACKTRACE` | Enable backtraces on errors | `1` |\n\n## Debug Levels (RUST_LOG Settings)\n\n### 1. minimal - INFO Level\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n```\n**Purpose:** High-level strand operations only  \n**Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification  \n**Output:** Evaluation start/end, final results only\n\n### 2. standard - DEBUG Level (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n**Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior  \n**Output:** All filtering operations, candidate counts, decisions\n\n### 3. detailed - TRACE Level\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Complete execution details  \n**Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow  \n**Output:** Every step of the decision process with full details\n\n### 4. comprehensive - Multi-module TRACE\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG  \n**Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions  \n**Output:** Pluck trace + bead store queries, worker lifecycle\n\n### 5. full - All NEEDLE modules\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n**Use case:** Complete system debugging  \n**Output:** All major NEEDLE subsystems\n\n### 6. maximum - Global TRACE\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Everything at TRACE level  \n**Use case:** Deep system-level debugging  \n**Output:** All Rust modules at TRACE (very verbose)\n\n## Complete Command Examples\n\n### Basic Example (Standard Debug)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### With Comprehensive Logging\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n### With Multiple Workers\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 3 2>&1 | tee pluck-multi-worker.log\n```\n\n### With Agent Specification\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -a claude -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-claude.log\n```\n\n### With Timeout\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -t 300 -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-timeout.log\n```\n\n### With Custom Identifier\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -i \"debug-worker-1\" -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-custom.log\n```\n\n## Using the Configuration Script\n\nA convenience script is available at `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh`:\n\n```bash\n# Standard debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log standard\n\n# Comprehensive debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log comprehensive\n\n# Detailed trace level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log detailed\n\n# Full system debugging\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log full\n\n# Maximum verbosity\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log maximum\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output\n\n### 1. Strand Evaluation Start\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n### 3. Query Results\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by excluded labels\n  excluded_beads=[\"bf-1234\", \"bf-5678\"]\n  reasons=[\"label:deferred\", \"label:blocked\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by status and assignee\n  remaining=3\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting candidates by priority\n  first_candidate=\"bf-abcd\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Decision\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Check: split_threshold\n  failure_count=2\n  split_threshold=3\n  should_split=false\n```\n\n### 8. Final Result\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Strand evaluation complete\n  result=BeadFound(\"bf-abcd\")\n```\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n```bash\n# View all Pluck-related events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Filter specific decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# View candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Count events by type\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Configuration Script Internals\n\nThe configuration script (`pluck-debug-config.sh`) encapsulates these preset RUST_LOG values:\n\n```bash\n# Preset definitions\ndeclare -A PRESETS=(\n    [\"minimal\"]=\"needle::strand::pluck=info\"\n    [\"standard\"]=\"needle::strand::pluck=debug\"\n    [\"detailed\"]=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace\"\n    [\"comprehensive\"]=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\"\n    [\"full\"]=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\"\n    [\"maximum\"]=\"trace\"\n)\n```\n\n## Command Syntax Verification\n\n### Against NEEDLE Documentation\n\nThe command structure has been verified against:\n- `needle --help` output\n- `needle run --help` output\n- NEEDLE README.md existing `pluck-debug-config.sh` script\n- Aligns with existing documentation in workspace\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Met\n\n✅ Complete Pluck command with debug flags documented  \n✅ Command syntax verified against Pluck source code  \n✅ Command ready for execution  \n✅ Comprehensive reference created for future use\n\n---\n\n# Complete Pluck Debug Command Structure\n\n## Core Command Structure\n\n### Base Command\n```bash\nneedle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n### With Debug Logging\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> 2>&1 | tee <output_file>\n```\n\n## Command Options (needle run)\n\n| Option | Short | Parameter | Description | Default |\n|--------|-------|-----------|-------------|---------|\n| `--workspace` | `-w` | `<PATH>` | Workspace directory to process beads from | Required |\n| `--agent` | `-a` | `<NAME>` | Agent adapter to use | Config default |\n| `--count` | `-c` | `<NUMBER>` | Number of workers to launch | 1 |\n| `--identifier` | `-i` | `<ID>` | Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming) | Auto-generated |\n| `--timeout` | `-t` | `<SECONDS>` | Agent execution timeout | Config default |\n| `--resume` | | | Resume existing worker session | false |\n| `--hot-reload` | | `true\\|false` | Enable hot-reload for this worker | Config default |\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Examples |\n|----------|---------|----------|\n| `RUST_LOG` | Controls Rust crate-level logging | `needle::strand::pluck=debug` |\n| `RUST_BACKTRACE` | Enable backtraces on errors | `1` |\n\n## Complete Command Examples\n\n### Basic Example (Standard Debug)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### With Comprehensive Logging\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n### With Multiple Workers\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 3 2>&1 | tee pluck-multi-worker.log\n```\n\n### With Agent Specification\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -a claude -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-claude.log\n```\n\n### With Timeout\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -t 300 -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-timeout.log\n```\n\n### With Custom Identifier\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n  needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -i \"debug-worker-1\" -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-custom.log\n```\n\n## Configuration Script Usage\n\nThe `pluck-debug-config.sh` script provides convenient preset configurations:\n\n```bash\n# Standard debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log standard\n\n# Comprehensive debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log comprehensive\n\n# Detailed trace level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log detailed\n\n# Full system debugging\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log full\n\n# Maximum verbosity\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log maximum\n```\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n```bash\n# View all Pluck-related events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Filter specific decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Count events by type\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Summary\n\nThe complete Pluck debug command structure provides:\n\n✅ **Base command:** `needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>`  \n✅ **Debug control:** Via `RUST_LOG` environment variable  \n✅ **Six preset levels:** From minimal to maximum verbosity  \n✅ **Flexible options:** Agent selection, timeout, custom identifiers  \n✅ **Output capture:** Standard shell redirection with `tee`  \n✅ **Analysis tools:** Grep-based log filtering and counting  \n✅ **Script automation:** `pluck-debug-config.sh` for convenience\n\nAll commands have been verified against NEEDLE 0.2.11 documentation and source
